title: Force-push only with --force-with-lease
description: When a push must rewrite remote history, use the lease variant so a teammate's intervening commits abort the push instead of being silently lost.
body: Use when a rebase or amend means the remote branch must be updated to a rewritten history. Do not use a plain --force, which overwrites whatever is on the remote regardless of new commits. Actions: fetch first; verify the remote tip is the commit you expect; push with --force-with-lease so the push fails if someone else advanced the branch. Validation signals include a push that aborts when the remote moved, and a remote tip that matches your rebased branch afterward. Risk boundary: any history rewrite on a shared branch is destructive and needs explicit human confirmation before the push.
